title: Loki and the Holy Knights
description: On pages 2–4, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Giants as The Elbaf conflict sharpens around Loki, the God’s Knights, giant children, and the secrets of the island’s royal past.
scene: The Elbaf conflict sharpens around Loki, the God’s Knights, giant children, and the secrets of the island’s royal past.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They say he's been here for hundreds of years.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All we can say for sure about this owl is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: BIBELOT (head librarian of the owl library)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: News COOS don't come to Elbaf since it's not part of the world government,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But sometimes the fishermen bring some tiny papers back here so we can read them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He must have eaten the rear-rear fruit and become a nurturing owl!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to think even inorganic things can grow with his power!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's a perfect ability for a kingdom of giants!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I hope they have medical books here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I know we had a few, but most of it was archaeological and historical works.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We got some medical books native to Elbaf, too!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wow!! I can't wait to read them!:)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Although, those books were always large, so they can't be shrunk down...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Can't you see that you're scaring them?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hey, I wanted it to stop, too...

summary: This part advances the Loki and the Holy Knights through 35 cleaned dialogue beats, emphasizing the core conflict, character stakes, and transition into the next movement of the story.
